papers.title: AudioChat: Unified Audio Storytelling, Editing, and Understanding with Transfusion Forcing
papers.titleJa: AudioChat: 統一された音声ストーリーテリング、編集、理解を実現するトランスフュージョンフォーシング
papers.what: 音声ストーリー（複数話者や背景音を含む複雑な音響シーン）を生成、編集、理解するためのフレームワークAudioChatを提案。
papers.novel: LLMベースのツール呼び出しエージェントを活用し、シミュレートされた対話をトレーニングデータとして使用する新しいパラダイムを導入。
papers.method: Audio Transfusion Forcingという新しい目的関数を導入し、構造化された思考の連鎖を通じて高レベルの指示を分解し、対話型のマルチターン音声理解と生成を実現。
papers.validation: 生成と編集性能を評価するために、新たに3つのタスク指向の評価指標を開発し、デモを公開。
papers.discussion: 複雑な音響シーンにおけるモデルの汎化性能や、現実世界での適用可能性が今後の課題。

nextReads.label: Whisper: OpenAI's Automatic Speech Recognition System (2022)
AudioLM: a Language Modeling Approach to Audio Generation (2022)
Whispering to Large Language Models (2023)
SoundStorm: Efficient Parallel Audio Generation (2023)

papers.abstract: Despite recent breakthroughs, audio foundation models struggle in processing complex multi-source acoustic scenes. We refer to this challenging domain as audio stories, which can have multiple speakers and background/foreground sound effects. Compared to traditional audio processing tasks, audio stories introduce new layers of semantic, temporal, and physical complexity. To address this challenge, we propose AudioChat, a framework for developing audio foundation models that can generate, edit, and understand audio stories. AudioChat introduces a new paradigm in which LLM-based toolcalling agents simulate interactions between users and the system, and these simulated dialogues are used as training data. We also introduce a novel Audio Transfusion Forcing objective to train the AudioChat model, allowing it to simultaneously decompose high-level instructions via structured chain-of-thought reasoning and perform interactive multi-turn audio understanding/generation. To evaluate generation and editing performance, we develop three new metrics that directly measure task performance instead of relying upon distribution-based scoring. We highly encourage readers to visit our demo to better understand the capabilities of AudioChat: https://wanchichen.github.io/audiochat/.

papers.title: Scaling Open Discrete Audio Foundation Models with Interleaved Semantic, Acoustic, and Text Tokens
papers.titleJa: セマンティック、音響、テキストトークンを組み合わせたオープンな離散音声基盤モデルのスケーリング
papers.what: 音声、音響、テキストを統合的にモデル化する離散音声基盤モデルのスケーリングに関する体系的な研究。
papers.novel: 離散音声モデルにおけるスケーリング則を初めて調査し、データ量とモデルサイズの最適な関係を明らかにした。
papers.method: 次トークン予測を用い、セマンティック、音響、テキストトークンを統合的に処理するアーキテクチャを採用し、SODAモデルを構築。
papers.validation: 135Mから4Bパラメータのモデルを500Bトークンでトレーニングし、スケーリング予測や既存モデルと比較。
papers.discussion: モデルのスケーリングにおけるデータと計算リソースのトレードオフや、さらなる汎化性能の向上が課題。

nextReads.label: AudioLM: a Language Modeling Approach to Audio Generation (2022)
Whisper: OpenAI's Automatic Speech Recognition System (2022)
SoundStream: An End-to-End Neural Audio Codec (2021)
Semantic Tokenization for Speech Synthesis (2022)

papers.abstract: Current audio language models are predominantly text-first, either extending pre-trained text LLM backbones or relying on semantic-only audio tokens, limiting general audio modeling. This paper presents a systematic empirical study of native audio foundation models that apply next-token prediction to audio at scale, jointly modeling semantic content, acoustic details, and text to support both general audio generation and cross-modal capabilities. We provide comprehensive empirical insights for building such models: (1) We systematically investigate design choices -- data sources, text mixture ratios, and token composition -- establishing a validated training recipe. (2) We conduct the first scaling law study for discrete audio models via IsoFLOP analysis on 64 models spanning $3{\times}10^{18}$ to $3{\times}10^{20}$ FLOPs, finding that optimal data grows 1.6$\times$ faster than optimal model size. (3) We apply these lessons to train SODA (Scaling Open Discrete Audio), a suite of models from 135M to 4B parameters on 500B tokens, comparing against our scaling predictions and existing models. SODA serves as a flexible backbone for diverse audio/text tasks -- we demonstrate this by fine-tuning for voice-preserving speech-to-speech translation, using the same unified architecture.

papers.title: MAEB: Massive Audio Embedding Benchmark
papers.titleJa: MAEB: 大規模音声埋め込みベンチマーク
papers.what: 音声、音楽、環境音、音声テキスト推論を含む30のタスクを対象とした大規模音声埋め込みベンチマークMAEBを提案。
papers.novel: 100以上の言語をカバーする30タスクを統一評価するベンチマークを構築し、50以上のモデルを評価して性能の特徴を明らかにした。
papers.method: MAEB+という98タスクのコレクションから多様性を維持しつつ評価コストを削減したMAEBを作成し、MTEBエコシステムに統合。
papers.validation: 音声エンコーダの性能がMAEBでの評価と音声大規模言語モデルでの性能に高い相関があることを示した。
papers.discussion: 音響理解と言語タスクの両方で優れた性能を持つモデルの開発が課題。

nextReads.label: HEAR 2021: Holistic Evaluation of Audio Representations (2021)
Wav2Vec 2.0: A Framework for Self-Supervised Learning of Speech Representations (2020)
CLAP: Learning Audio Concepts from Natural Language Supervision (2022)
AudioCLIP: Extending CLIP to Image, Text and Audio (2021)

papers.abstract: We introduce the Massive Audio Embedding Benchmark (MAEB), a large-scale benchmark covering 30 tasks across speech, music, environmental sounds, and cross-modal audio-text reasoning in 100+ languages. We evaluate 50+ models and find that no single model dominates across all tasks: contrastive audio-text models excel at environmental sound classification (e.g., ESC50) but score near random on multilingual speech tasks (e.g., SIB-FLEURS), while speech-pretrained models show the opposite pattern. Clustering remains challenging for all models, with even the best-performing model achieving only modest results. We observe that models excelling on acoustic understanding often perform poorly on linguistic tasks, and vice versa. We also show that the performance of audio encoders on MAEB correlates highly with their performance when used in audio large language models. MAEB is derived from MAEB+, a collection of 98 tasks. MAEB is designed to maintain task diversity while reducing evaluation cost, and it integrates into the MTEB ecosystem for unified evaluation across text, image, and audio modalities. We release MAEB and all 98 tasks along with code and a leaderboard at https://github.com/embeddings-benchmark/mteb.

papers.title: TAC: Timestamped Audio Captioning
papers.titleJa: TAC: タイムスタンプ付き音声キャプショニング
papers.what: ① 複雑な音響シーンにおける重なり合うイベントを識別し、時間的に一貫した音声キャプションを生成するモデルTACを提案。
papers.novel: ② 合成データパイプラインを用いて現実的な多声条件下での学習を可能にし、低い幻覚率と正確な時間的基盤を実現。
papers.method: ③ 実世界の音源から作成した動的な音声ミックスを用いてモデルを訓練し、時間的に整合性のあるキャプションを生成。
papers.validation: ④ MMAU-ProやMMSUなどのベンチマークで、TACとTAC-Vが最先端のスコアを達成し、競合手法を上回る性能を確認。
papers.discussion: ⑤ 合成データに依存しているため、実際のデータでの性能や一般化能力についてさらなる検証が必要。

nextReads.label: Audio Captioning: A Comprehensive Review (2022)
Audio-Visual Scene Analysis with Transformers (2021)
Dense-Captioning Events in Videos (2017)
Audio-Visual Scene-Aware Dialog (2020)

papers.abstract: Large Audio Language Models struggle to disentangle overlapping events in complex acoustic scenes, yielding temporally inconsistent captions and frequent hallucinations. We introduce Timestamped Audio Captioner (TAC), a model that produces temporally grounded audio descriptions at varying degrees of detail and resolution. TAC is trained with a synthetic data pipeline that constructs challenging and dynamic mixtures from real-world audio sources, enabling robust learning under realistic polyphonic conditions. Across event detection and dense captioning, TAC outperforms all competing methods, with a low hallucination rate and accurate temporal grounding. We also introduce TAC-V, an audio-visual pipeline to generate semantically rich audio-visual descriptions. We then show that TAC and TAC-V serves as a "semantic bridge" for a text-only reasoner: a simple TAC$\rightarrow$LLM and TAC-V$\rightarrow$LLM cascade achieves state-of-the-art scores on benchmarks for both audio (MMAU-Pro, MMSU, MMAR) and audio-visual (DailyOmni, VideoHolmes) understanding and reasoning respectively.

papers.title: What Do Neurons Listen To? A Neuron-level Dissection of a General-purpose Audio Model
papers.titleJa: ニューロンは何を聴いているのか？汎用音声モデルのニューロンレベル解析
papers.what: ① 汎用音声自己教師あり学習モデルの内部表現をニューロンレベルで解析し、そのメカニズムを明らかにする研究。
papers.novel: ② クラス特異的なニューロンの出現を特定し、それらが新しいタスククラスに対しても広範なカバレッジを提供することを示した。
papers.method: ③ ニューロンの条件付き活性化パターンを解析し、音声特徴や音楽的ピッチなどの意味的カテゴリ間で共有される応答を明らかに。
papers.validation: ④ 分類性能に対するニューロンの機能的影響を確認し、SSLモデルの内部表現の有効性を実証。
papers.discussion: ⑤ ニューロンレベルでの解析に基づくが、モデル全体の挙動や他の要因との相互作用についてのさらなる研究が必要。

nextReads.label: Self-Supervised Learning for Audio: A Review (2021)
Understanding Neural Networks Through Representations (2020)
Exploring the Role of Neurons in Deep Learning (2019)
Mechanistic Interpretability of Neural Networks (2022)

papers.abstract: In this paper, we analyze the internal representations of a general-purpose audio self-supervised learning (SSL) model from a neuron-level perspective. Despite their strong empirical performance as feature extractors, the internal mechanisms underlying the robust generalization of SSL audio models remain unclear. Drawing on the framework of mechanistic interpretability, we identify and examine class-specific neurons by analyzing conditional activation patterns across diverse tasks. Our analysis reveals that SSL models foster the emergence of class-specific neurons that provide extensive coverage across novel task classes. These neurons exhibit shared responses across different semantic categories and acoustic similarities, such as speech attributes and musical pitch. We also confirm that these neurons have a functional impact on classification performance. To our knowledge, this is the first systematic neuron-level analysis of a general-purpose audio SSL model, providing new insights into its internal representation.

papers.title: Hello-Chat: Towards Realistic Social Audio Interactions
papers.titleJa: Hello-Chat: リアルな社会的音声対話の実現に向けて
papers.what: ① リアルな社会的シナリオにおける自然な音声対話を可能にするエンドツーエンドの音声言語モデルHello-Chatを提案。
papers.novel: ② 実生活の会話データセットとモダリティを交互に訓練する戦略を活用し、人間らしい生成と感情的な共鳴を実現。
papers.method: ③ 大規模な実生活会話データセットを用い、モダリティ交互訓練戦略を採用して、音声の自然さと感情的な一致を向上。
papers.validation: ④ 音声理解タスクでSOTA性能を達成し、韻律の自然さや感情的な一致で既存のベースラインを大幅に上回ることを実証。
papers.discussion: ⑤ 実生活での多様なシナリオにおける性能や、より複雑な感情表現への対応が今後の課題。

nextReads.label: Empathetic Dialogue Systems (2021)
Conversational AI for Social Interaction (2020)
Prosody in Speech Synthesis (2019)
Large Audio Language Models: A Survey (2022)

papers.abstract: Recent advancements in Large Audio Language Models (LALMs) have demonstrated exceptional performance in speech recognition and translation. However, existing models often suffer from a disconnect between perception and expression, resulting in a robotic "read-speech" style that lacks the spontaneity and emotional resonance of real human interaction. In this report, we introduce Hello-Chat, an end-to-end audio language model designed for realistic social scenarios. By leveraging a massive dataset of real-life conversations and employing a modality-interleaved training strategy, Hello-Chat achieves a breakthrough in anthropomorphic generation. Experimental results show that our model not only reaches state-of-the-art (SOTA) performance on specific audio understanding tasks but also significantly outperforms existing baselines in prosodic naturalness and emotional alignment, paving the way for the next generation of empathetic AI agents.

papers.title: The Interspeech 2026 Audio Reasoning Challenge: Evaluating Reasoning Process Quality for Audio Reasoning Models and Agents
papers.titleJa: Interspeech 2026 オーディオ推論チャレンジ: オーディオ推論モデルとエージェントの推論プロセス品質の評価
papers.what: ① 音声推論モデルの透明性向上を目指し、オーディオ推論におけるChain-of-Thought（CoT）品質を評価する初の共有タスクを提案。
papers.novel: ② MMAR-Rubricsという新しいプロトコルを導入し、事実性と論理性をインスタンスレベルで評価する方法を開発。
papers.method: ③ シングルモデルとエージェントトラックを設け、反復的なツールの統合やクロスモーダル分析を活用して推論品質を向上。
papers.validation: ④ 156チームによる競争結果を分析し、エージェントシステムが推論品質でリードしていることを確認。
papers.discussion: ⑤ シングルモデルの性能向上が見られるが、さらなる強化学習やデータパイプラインの改善が必要。

nextReads.label: Chain-of-Thought Prompting Elicits Reasoning in LLMs (2022)
Explainable AI for Audio (2021)
Cross-Modal Reasoning in AI Systems (2020)
Evaluating Reasoning in AI Models (2021)

papers.abstract: Recent Large Audio Language Models (LALMs) excel in understanding but often lack transparent reasoning. To address this "black-box" limitation, we organized the Audio Reasoning Challenge at Interspeech 2026, the first shared task dedicated to evaluating Chain-of-Thought (CoT) quality in the audio domain. The challenge introduced MMAR-Rubrics, a novel instance-level protocol assessing the factuality and logic of reasoning chains. Featured Single Model and Agent tracks, the competition attracting 156 teams from 18 countries and regions. Results show agent systems currently lead in reasoning quality, utilizing iterative tool orchestration and cross-modal analysis. Besides, single models are rapidly advancing via reinforcement learning and sophisticated data pipeline. We details the challenge design, methodology, and a comprehensive analysis of state-of-the-art systems, providing new insights for explainable audio intelligence.
papers.comment: The official website of the Audio Reasoning Challenge: https://audio-reasoning-challenge.github.io

papers.title: Eureka-Audio: Triggering Audio Intelligence in Compact Language Models
papers.titleJa: Eureka-Audio: コンパクトな言語モデルによる音声知能の引き出し
papers.what: ① 1.7Bパラメータの軽量な音声言語モデルEureka-Audioを提案し、ASRや音声理解タスクで大規模モデルに匹敵する性能を実現。
papers.novel: ② Mixture-of-Experts（MoE）アダプターを活用し、音声の多様性に対応しつつ、計算コストを抑えた高性能モデルを開発。
papers.method: ③ Whisperベースの音声エンコーダと軽量な言語バックボーン、MoEアダプターを組み合わせた統一的なエンドツーエンドアーキテクチャを採用。
papers.validation: ④ ASR、知識推論、安全性、指示追従、パラリンガル推論のベンチマークで大規模モデルを上回る性能を実証。
papers.discussion: ⑤ モデルの軽量化と性能のバランスを達成したが、さらなる汎用性の向上が課題。

nextReads.label: Mixture of Experts in Deep Learning (2021)
Whisper: OpenAI's Speech Recognition Model (2022)
Efficient Audio Models (2020)
Audio Instruction Tuning for AI (2022)

papers.abstract: We present Eureka-Audio, a compact yet high-performance audio language model that achieves competitive performance against models that are 4 to 18 times larger across a broad range of audio understanding benchmarks. Despite containing only 1.7B parameters, Eureka-Audio demonstrates strong performance on automatic speech recognition (ASR), audio understanding, and dense audio captioning, matching or surpassing multiple 7B to 30B audio and omni-modal baselines. The model adopts a unified end-to-end architecture composed of a lightweight language backbone, a Whisper-based audio encoder, and a sparsely activated Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) adapter that explicitly accounts for audio heterogeneity and alleviates cross-modal optimization conflicts under limited capacity. To further enhance paralinguistic reasoning, we introduce DataFlux, a closed loop audio instruction data synthesis and verification pipeline that constructs high quality, logically consistent supervision from raw audio. Extensive evaluations across ASR, knowledge reasoning, safety, instruction following, and paralinguistic benchmarks, demonstrate that Eureka-Audio achieves an efficient balance between computational cost and performance. These results establish Eureka Audio as a strong and practical baseline for lightweight audio understanding models.

papers.title: Audiocards: Structured Metadata Improves Audio Language Models For Sound Design
papers.titleJa: Audiocards: 構造化メタデータによるサウンドデザイン向け音声言語モデルの改善
papers.what: ① サウンドデザインにおける音声検索を改善するため、音響属性や音響記述子に基づいた構造化メタデータ「Audiocards」を提案し、その効果を検証した研究です。
papers.novel: ② 従来の単一文キャプション方式を超え、音響属性に基づいた構造化メタデータを活用することで、音声キャプション生成やテキスト-音声検索の性能を向上させた点が新規性です。
papers.method: ③ LLM（大規模言語モデル）の知識を活用して、音響属性や音響記述子に基づいた「Audiocards」を生成し、これを用いて音声キャプション生成や検索タスクを学習しました。
papers.validation: ④ プロフェッショナルなサウンドエフェクトライブラリを用いた実験で、テキスト-音声検索、キャプション生成、メタデータ生成の性能向上を確認しました。また、一般的な音声キャプション生成や検索タスクでもベースラインを上回る結果を示しました。
papers.discussion: ⑤ Audiocardsの有効性は示されたものの、特定の音響属性や記述子の選定基準や、他の音声データセットへの適用可能性についてはさらなる研究が必要です。

nextReads.label: AudioCaps: Generating Captions for Audios (2019)
Clotho: An Audio Captioning Dataset (2020)
Wav2CLIP: Learning Robust Audio Representations From CLIP (2022)
Audio Retrieval with Natural Language Queries (2021)

papers.abstract: Sound designers search for sounds in large sound effects libraries using aspects such as sound class or visual context. However, the metadata needed for such search is often missing or incomplete, and requires significant manual effort to add. Existing solutions to automate this task by generating metadata, i.e. captioning, and search using learned embeddings, i.e. text-audio retrieval, are not trained on metadata with the structure and information pertinent to sound design. To this end we propose audiocards, structured metadata grounded in acoustic attributes and sonic descriptors, by exploiting the world knowledge of LLMs. We show that training on audiocards improves downstream text-audio retrieval, descriptive captioning, and metadata generation on professional sound effects libraries. Moreover, audiocards also improve performance on general audio captioning and retrieval over the baseline single-sentence captioning approach. We release a curated dataset of sound effects audiocards to invite further research in audio language modeling for sound design.

papers.title: AuTAgent: A Reinforcement Learning Framework for Tool-Augmented Audio Reasoning
papers.titleJa: AuTAgent: ツール拡張型音声推論のための強化学習フレームワーク
papers.what: ① 音声言語モデル（LALMs）の推論能力を強化するため、外部ツールを適切に活用する強化学習フレームワーク「AuTAgent」を提案した研究です。
papers.novel: ② 外部ツールの選択と使用タイミングを学習する強化学習戦略を導入し、LALMsの表現力の限界を補完する点が新規性です。
papers.method: ③ Sparse-feedbackトレーニングと新しい差分報酬メカニズムを採用し、文脈に応じて最適なツールを選択・活用するエージェントを訓練しました。
papers.validation: ④ MMAU Test-miniとMMARベンチマークで、オープンソースおよびクローズドソースのバックボーンモデルに対し、精度がそれぞれ4.20% / 6.20%および9.80% / 8.00%向上することを確認しました。
papers.discussion: ⑤ 外部ツールの選択と使用が有効であることを示しましたが、ツールの選定基準や計算コストの最適化など、さらなる研究が必要です。

nextReads.label: Toolformer: Language Models Can Teach Themselves to Use Tools (2023)
Audio Spectrogram Transformer (2021)
Self-Supervised Learning for General-Purpose Audio Representations (2021)
HEAR: Holistic Evaluation of Audio Representations (2021)

papers.abstract: Large Audio Language Models (LALMs) excel at perception but struggle with complex reasoning requiring precise acoustic measurements. While external tools can extract fine-grained features like exact tempo or pitch, effective integration remains challenging: naively using all tools causes information overload, while prompt-based selection fails to assess context-dependent utility. To address this, we propose AuTAgent (Audio Tool Agent), a reinforcement learning framework that learns when and which tools to invoke. By employing a sparse-feedback training strategy with a novel Differential Reward mechanism, the agent learns to filter out irrelevant tools and invokes external assistance only when it yields a net performance gain over the base model. Experimental results confirm that AuTAgent complements the representation bottleneck of LALMs by providing verifiable acoustic evidence. It improves accuracy by 4.20% / 6.20% and 9.80% / 8.00% for open-source and closed-source backbones on the MMAU Test-mini and the MMAR benchmarks, respectively. In addition, further experiments demonstrate exceptional transferability. We highlight the complementary role of external tools in augmenting audio model reasoning.

papers.title: Spatial Audio Question Answering and Reasoning on Dynamic Source Movements
papers.titleJa: 動的な音源移動に基づく空間音声質問応答と推論
papers.what: 動的な音源移動を含む複雑な音響シーンを理解するための空間音声質問応答（Spatial AQA）を研究。
papers.novel: 動き中心の空間音声拡張フレームワークを提案し、制御可能でスケーラブルなトレーニングデータ生成を実現。
papers.method: 音声と言語のマルチモーダルなファインチューニング手法を採用し、推論前に中間的な思考ステップを生成する「思考モード」を導入。
papers.validation: 動き推論の精度を評価し、特に単一イベントの質問において思考モードが+5.1%の改善を示した。
papers.discussion: 複雑な音響シーンでの推論精度向上や、実世界での適用可能性が課題として残る。

nextReads.label: Listen to Learn: Audio-Visual Object Localization (2021)
Audio-Visual Scene Analysis with Self-Supervised Multisensory Features (2020)
Learning to Separate Object Sounds by Watching Unlabeled Video (2018)
Self-Supervised Learning of Audio-Visual Objects from Video (2021)

papers.abstract: Spatial audio understanding aims to enable machines to interpret complex auditory scenes, particularly when sound sources move over time. In this work, we study Spatial Audio Question Answering (Spatial AQA) with a focus on movement reasoning, where a model must infer object motion, position, and directional changes directly from stereo audio. First, we introduce a movement-centric spatial audio augmentation framework that synthesizes diverse motion patterns from isolated mono audio events, enabling controlled and scalable training data generation. Second, we propose an end-to-end multimodal finetuning approach with a thinking mode, which allows audio-language models to produce explicit intermediate reasoning steps before predicting an answer. Third, we investigate the impact of query-conditioned source separation as a preprocessing stage and compare three inference regimes: no masking, an audio grounding model (AGM), and ground-truth masks. Our results show that reasoning amplifies the benefits of source separation, with thinking mode showing significant improvement of +5.1% when a single event is present in the question. These findings highlight the interplay between movement modeling, reasoning, and separation quality, offering new insights for advancing spatial audio understanding.

papers.title: How Much Does Machine Identity Matter in Anomalous Sound Detection at Test Time?
papers.titleJa: 異常音検出におけるテスト時の機械識別の重要性
papers.what: 異常音検出（ASD）において、テスト時に機械識別が利用できない場合の性能劣化とロバスト性の違いを調査。
papers.novel: 従来の機械別評価プロトコルを修正し、複数機械の録音を統合して評価する新しいプロトコルを提案。
papers.method: 機械識別なしでの評価を行い、異常音検出手法の性能劣化とロバスト性の違いを分析。
papers.validation: 代表的なASD手法を用いて実験を行い、従来の評価では見えなかった性能劣化やロバスト性の違いを明らかにした。
papers.discussion: 現実的なシナリオでのASDの性能向上や、機械識別を必要としない手法の開発が課題。

nextReads.label: Anomalous Sound Detection based on Deep Learning (2020)
DCASE 2020 Challenge Task 2: Unsupervised Detection of Anomalous Sounds (2020)
Anomalous Sound Detection Based on Autoencoder with Decision Boundary (2021)
Deep Feature Learning for Anomalous Sound Detection (2021)

papers.abstract: Anomalous sound detection (ASD) benchmarks typically assume that the identity of the monitored machine is known at test time and that recordings are evaluated in a machine-wise manner. However, in realistic monitoring scenarios with multiple known machines operating concurrently, test recordings may not be reliably attributable to a specific machine, and requiring machine identity imposes deployment constraints such as dedicated sensors per machine. To reveal performance degradations and method-specific differences in robustness that are hidden under standard machine-wise evaluation, we consider a minimal modification of the ASD evaluation protocol in which test recordings from multiple machines are merged and evaluated jointly without access to machine identity at inference time. Training data and evaluation metrics remain unchanged, and machine identity labels are used only for post hoc evaluation. Experiments with representative ASD methods show that relaxing this assumption reveals performance degradations and method-specific differences in robustness that are hidden under standard machine-wise evaluation, and that these degradations are strongly related to implicit machine identification accuracy.

trend: ① 音声、音響、テキストを統合的に扱う基盤モデルや埋め込みベンチマーク（MAEB、離散音声モデル）のスケーリングと評価が進展。
② 音響シーン理解や推論能力を向上させる新しいフレームワーク（AudioChat、Spatial AQA、AuTAgent）や課題（Interspeech 2026 Challenge）が提案。
③ 音声キャプション生成（TAC）、音声対話（Hello-Chat）、音声検索（Audiocards）など、応用領域でのモデル性能向上を目指した研究が活発化。
